heading: Cost limitations
label: 33105 Cost limitations
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)3310515:2024., , , § 604; added , , .Oct. 20, 1972Oct. 25, 1984Pub. L. 92–51386 Stat. 947Pub. L. 98–547, § 101(a)98 Stat. 2758In subsection (a)(1) and (2), the words “compliance costs” are substituted for “costs . . . to comply with such standard” to eliminate unnecessary words. In clause (2), the words “reasonable amount (but less than $15)” are substituted for “reasonable lesser amount” for clarity.In subsection (c)(2), the words “commencing on or after ” are omitted as obsolete.January 1, 1985

children.text: on a manufacturer of motor vehicles, compliance costs of more than $15 a motor vehicle; or
on a manufacturer of major replacement parts, compliance costs for each part of more than the reasonable amount (but less than $15) that the Secretary of Transportation specifies in the standard.

children.heading: .—Costs Involved in Engines and Transmissions
children.label: b .—Costs Involved in Engines and Transmissions

children.text: the costs of identifying engines and transmissions may not be considered in calculating the manufacturer’s costs under subsection (a) of this section; and

the manufacturer may not be required under the standard to conform to any identification system for engines and transmissions that imposes greater costs on the manufacturer than are incurred under the identification system used by the manufacturer on .October 25, 1984

children.label: B
children.text: “price index” means the average over a calendar year of the Consumer Price Index (all items—United States city average) published monthly by the Secretary of Labor.

At the beginning of each calendar year, as necessary data become available from the Bureau of Labor Statistics, the Secretary of Labor shall certify to the Secretary of Transportation and publish in the Federal Register the percentage difference between the price index for the 12 months before the beginning of the calendar year and the price index for the base period. For model years beginning in that calendar year, the amounts specified in subsection (a) of this section shall be adjusted by the percentage difference.
